title: Drops In, Drops Out
description: Packet runs a pharmacy in data-terms. Daemon tries to make sense of it. Affectionate absurdism.

steps.text: The stall had a faded sign: DROPS, LOST AND FOUND. The proprietor was small, fast, and operated a cash register with six buttons, none of them numbered.

lines.speaker: Packet
lines.text: Retransmission rate. Or round-trip time if you prefer. I've got tinctures for both.

lines.speaker: Daemon
lines.text: I'm not sure I have either of those problems.

lines.speaker: Packet
lines.text: Oh, you have them. Everyone has them. Some people just don't measure.

lines.speaker: Packet
lines.text: The big sellers this week are the duplicate-ACK salve and the fragmentation tonic.

lines.speaker: Packet
lines.text: Look, the apothecary on the analog side uses herbs. We use what we have. What we have is protocols.

lines.speaker: Packet
lines.text: Placebo has a 34% clinical efficacy. That's in print. I'm not reinventing medicine.

lines.speaker: Packet
lines.text: Also, I give good advice. Free with purchase of anything over one credit.

lines.speaker: Packet
lines.text: Stop retransmitting. The other side hears you the first time. They're just slow.

steps.text: The Admin bought a duplicate-ACK salve. Nobody used it. It sat in the inventory for eleven chapters, occasionally remembered, occasionally inspected, a reminder that the world had edges and some of them ran small shops.
